title: Add Signal Cal.com meeting link
description: Context: User asked to analyze https://cal.com/bogorad and add the Signal meeting to the corresponding site block. Evidence from the public Cal.com page: it lists Meeting (Signal), 60 m, at https://cal.com/bogorad/meeting-signal. Current site state: content/_index.md Talk block has Zoom and Meet links only. Desired state: Talk block includes a Signal meeting link with a label matching existing style. Verification: Hugo build passes, generated HTML contains the Signal link, and Chrome-CDP checks at required screen sizes show no horizontal overflow/readability regressions.
close_reason: Signal Cal.com meeting link added and verified; stale fallback CSS removed.

Verification: clean Hugo build; generated HTML contains Signal/Zoom/Meet links; rg found no talk-fallback / booking-or-chat text after clearing .temp/hugo-public; CDP on port 9222 checked 1280x900, 820x1180, 430x932, 414x896, 390x844, 375x667, 360x740, 320x568 with no overflow, no talk-link overflow, fallbackText=false, fallbackClass=0. title: Run Hugo and dependency-removal checks
description: Scope: verification only, after implementation changes. Run Hugo build to ./.temp/hugo-public and use ripgrep to confirm generated output has no Font Awesome, webfont, or script dependencies introduced by this change. Also inspect generated HTML enough to confirm content remains visible. Desired state: narrow static checks pass. Verification evidence to record in Beads comments: exact commands and pass/fail outcomes.
close_reason: Hugo/static checks passed with deprecation warnings fixed and no font/script dependencies.

comments.author: Eugene Bogorad
comments.text: Static build surfaced Hugo deprecations: config languageCode should become locale, and layouts/baseof.html should use .Language.Locale instead of .Language.LanguageCode. Fixing before final verification.

comments.author: Eugene Bogorad
comments.text: Static checks passed. Commands: hugo --cleanDestinationDir --destination ./.temp/hugo-public (no warnings after locale/.Language.Locale fix); rg 'fontawesome|fa-' ./.temp/hugo-public returned no matches; rg '<script|fontawesome|webfonts|\.woff|\.ttf' ./.temp/hugo-public returned no matches; generated index.html has 13 contact SVGs, 13 contact labels, the Talk fallback, and lang="en-US".

title: Convert contact icon data to stable keys
description: Scope: data/contacts.toml only. Change icon values from Font Awesome class strings to stable keys such as email, phone, whatsapp, telegram, rss, medium, linkedin, github, stack-overflow, and goodreads while preserving titles, hrefs, order, and external flags. Current evidence: data/contacts.toml currently has fa-solid/fa-brands icon values for 10 of 13 items and already has signal, x-twitter, and substack keys. Desired state: no fa-* strings remain in data/contacts.toml. Verification: rg "fa-|fontawesome" data/contacts.toml returns no matches.
close_reason: Contact icon data converted to stable keys; no fa/fontawesome refs remain in data.

comments.author: Eugene Bogorad
comments.text: Starting: update data/contacts.toml icon values to stable keys while preserving order, titles, hrefs, and external flags.

comments.author: Eugene Bogorad
comments.text: Completed. data/contacts.toml now uses stable icon keys for all contacts; rg 'fa-|fontawesome' data/contacts.toml returns no matches.

title: Phase 2: add visible contact labels
description: Goal: contact links remain identifiable even if icon rendering is impaired. Current state: layouts/_partials/home/contact-links.html renders icon-only anchors; assets/css/style.css styles them as 45px circular buttons. Desired state: each contact anchor includes a visible label span, and CSS supports icon plus text without overflow, layout jumps, or hover-only discoverability. Constraints: keep keyboard focus visible, avoid JavaScript, and tune desktop/tablet/mobile layouts without broad redesign. Verification: every contact item has visible non-hover text in at least one persistent page area; labels fit on mobile and desktop.
close_reason: Phase 2 source work complete: visible contact labels and responsive styles added.

title: Render all contact icons as inline SVG
description: Scope: layouts/_partials/home/contact-icon.html. Replace the current three-SVG-plus- fallback helper with key-based inline SVG rendering for every contact icon in data/contacts.toml. Current evidence: the partial handles signal, substack, and x-twitter, then emits for all other values. Desired state: every supported key emits an inline
close_reason: All configured contact keys render as inline SVG.

comments.author: Eugene Bogorad
comments.text: Starting: update layouts/_partials/home/contact-icon.html so every contact key renders inline SVG without Font Awesome fallback.

comments.author: Eugene Bogorad
comments.text: Completed. contact-icon partial renders inline SVG for every configured contact key; generated index.html contains 13 contact <svg> elements.

title: Phase 4: verify static Hugo output
description: Goal: prove the repo-level source changes remove icon-font dependencies and preserve generated content. Required checks: hugo --cleanDestinationDir --destination ./.temp/hugo-public; rg "fontawesome|fa-" ./.temp/hugo-public; rg "<script|fontawesome|webfonts|\\.woff|\\.ttf" ./.temp/hugo-public. Expected result: Hugo builds successfully; the built site has no Font Awesome or webfont references; existing name, bio, Talk links, interests, languages, timeline, contact labels, and fallback text remain in generated HTML. Constraints: report any skipped/unavailable checks honestly.
close_reason: Phase 4 complete: clean Hugo build and static dependency checks passed.

title: Phase 5: verify rendered layout through Chrome CDP
description: Goal: prove user-visible layout remains usable after the source changes. Required flow: run hugo server on localhost, connect to Chrome CDP port 9222 using scripts/cdp.mjs, inspect desktop/tablet/phone-width renderings, and confirm contact labels, icons, Talk fallback, focus states, and content are readable. Constraints: follow AGENTS.md and the Chrome-CDP skill; use CDP port 9222; do not use agent-browser for the live-site check; if no browser/CDP prompt can be approved, report the blocker. Manual Apple-device matrix (Normal Safari, Private Browsing, Locked Private Browsing, Lockdown Mode, common content blocker) remains outside local automation unless the user provides a device.
close_reason: Phase 5 complete: rendered layout verified through Chrome CDP at desktop, tablet, and multiple mobile resolutions.

title: Remove Font Awesome stylesheet and vendored files
description: Scope: layouts/_partials/head.html and static/vendor/fontawesome/6.4.0/. Remove the Font Awesome stylesheet link from the head partial and delete the now-unused vendored Font Awesome CSS/webfont files. Current evidence: layouts/_partials/head.html line 16 loads vendor/fontawesome/6.4.0/css/all.min.css; static/vendor/fontawesome/6.4.0 contains all.min.css and fa-*.ttf/.woff2 files. Desired state: no Font Awesome static assets remain in the repo, and head.html only links the Hugo Pipes stylesheet. Constraints: do not touch unrelated static assets. Verification: rg "fontawesome|fa-|\.woff|\.ttf" layouts static returns no Font Awesome dependency after icon replacement.
close_reason: Font Awesome stylesheet and vendored webfont assets removed.

Replace the circular icon-only styling with stable dimensions and wrapping rules that work for desktop side rail, tablet full-width row, and mobile. Current evidence: .contact-links a is fixed at 45px by 45px and has no text label. Desired state: labels are readable, do not overflow anchors, focus-visible state is clear, and layout remains polished. Constraints: keep card/sidebar structure intact and avoid nested cards. Verification: browser checks at desktop, tablet, and phone widths show readable labels and usable focus states.

title: Check desktop, tablet, and phone renderings with Chrome CDP
description: Scope: verification only. Start or reuse a local Hugo server, connect to Chrome over CDP port 9222 with scripts/cdp.mjs, navigate to the local site, and capture/inspect desktop, tablet, and phone-width states. Current repo note: README.md recommends hugo server --bind 127.0.0.1 --noBuildLock. Desired state: contact labels and icons are visible and non-overlapping, the Talk card and plain fallback are readable, main content remains visible, and keyboard focus can be observed or otherwise verified. Verification evidence to record: CDP commands used, target URL, viewport checks, screenshots if saved, and any limitations.
close_reason: CDP desktop/tablet/mobile viewport checks passed on port 9222.

comments.author: Eugene Bogorad
comments.text: Starting CDP verification. Will run a local Hugo server and inspect desktop/tablet/phone renderings through scripts/cdp.mjs on CDP port 9222.

comments.author: Eugene Bogorad
comments.text: CDP verification passed on port 9222 against http://127.0.0.1:1313/. Desktop 1280x900 and tablet 820x1180 had no horizontal overflow and no overflowing contact buttons. Mobile viewports tested: 320x568, 360x740, 375x667, 390x844, 414x896, 430x932; each reported pageWidth == clientWidth or no overflow, 13 contact SVGs, 13 contact labels, fallback visible, and no contact-button overflow. Focus check: Tab reached Email contact link with visible outline and accent shadow. Screenshots saved under .temp/cdp-desktop.png, .temp/cdp-tablet.png, .temp/cdp-phone.png, .temp/cdp-mobile-320x568.png, .temp/cdp-mobile-430x932.png, and .temp/cdp-focus-contact.png. Note: Hugo dev server injects a dev script; static build output separately verified no scripts/webfonts/Font Awesome references.

title: Make contact rail robust in strict browser modes
description: Context: The browser lockdown compatibility plan at .temp/browser-lockdown-recommendation-plan.md recommends removing contact-icon webfont dependencies, adding visible contact labels, adding a plain contact fallback near the Talk card, and verifying the Hugo output plus rendered layout. Motivation: Safari Private Browsing, Locked Private Browsing, Lockdown Mode, Private Relay, managed devices, and content blockers may impair web fonts or external destinations. The static Hugo site should remain understandable and contactable under those conditions. Current state: layouts/_partials/head.html loads static/vendor/fontawesome/6.4.0/css/all.min.css; data/contacts.toml contains fa-* icon classes for most contact items; layouts/_partials/home/contact-icon.html only inlines Signal, Substack, and X/Twitter before falling back to an <i> element; layouts/_partials/home/contact-links.html renders icon-only anchors; content/_index.md uses Cal.com links in the Talk section. Desired state: all contact icons render as inline SVG from stable icon keys, Font Awesome CSS and webfont files are gone, contact links have visible non-hover labels or a nearby visible fallback, and a plain email/phone fallback is visible without JavaScript. Evidence: rg "fontawesome|fa-" currently finds active matches in data/contacts.toml, layouts/_partials/head.html, and static/vendor/fontawesome/6.4.0; README.md says Hugo source is authoritative and public/ is generated only. Constraints: Follow AGENTS.md: use Beads and verify the live site through Chrome CDP on port 9222. Ignore the ccc-advice request; no ccc-advice work is in scope. Do not add JavaScript, analytics, trackers, SPA routing, or broad redesign. Keep edits focused to content/, data/, layouts/, assets/, and static/. Treat existing unrelated working-tree changes as user-owned. Verification contract: run Hugo/static checks, then run local rendered checks through scripts/cdp.mjs on CDP port 9222. Manual Apple-device Lockdown/Private Browsing checks remain a user-side matrix unless a physical device is available.
close_reason: Browser lockdown contact robustness work complete: implementation, static checks, deprecation fix, multi-resolution CDP checks, and AGENTS testing requirement done.

comments.author: Eugene Bogorad
comments.text: Starting execution locally: implementing icon-font removal, visible contact labels, and plain contact fallback before static/CDP verification. Existing unrelated working-tree changes are left untouched.

comments.author: Eugene Bogorad
comments.text: User requested a durable project-agent instruction for testing multiple screen resolutions. Updated AGENTS.md to require desktop/tablet/several mobile viewport checks, including small mobile, with overflow/readability/focus criteria and exact viewport/result recording.

title: Phase 1: remove Font Awesome icon font dependency
description: Goal: no contact icon depends on Font Awesome CSS or font files. Current state: layouts/_partials/head.html loads vendor/fontawesome/6.4.0/css/all.min.css, data/contacts.toml stores Font Awesome class strings for Email, Phone, WhatsApp, Telegram, Newsblur/RSS, Medium, LinkedIn, GitHub, Stack Overflow, and Goodreads, and static/vendor/fontawesome/6.4.0 contains CSS plus .ttf/.woff2 files. Desired state: data uses stable icon keys, layouts/_partials/home/contact-icon.html handles every key with inline SVG, and the vendored Font Awesome directory plus stylesheet link are removed. Constraints: keep SVGs decorative when visible text labels are present using aria-hidden="true" and focusable="false"; preserve anchor aria-label values from data/contacts.toml; use license-compatible/provenance-aware inline paths already present locally where possible. Verification: rg "fontawesome|fa-" layouts data static assets content should return no active dependency, and generated HTML should include inline SVG for every contact item.
close_reason: Phase 1 complete: icon data, inline SVG partial, and Font Awesome removal finished.

title: Phase 3: add plain contact fallback near Talk
description: Goal: users can still reach out when booking or chat services are blocked. Current state: content/_index.md Talk links point to Cal.com, and layouts/_partials/home/sidebar.html renders only the Talk link list, interests, and languages. Desired state: a short boring fallback line near the Talk card exposes email and phone/text without depending on Cal.com or social/chat domains. Constraints: keep the fallback concise, visible without JavaScript, and not noisy. Verification: generated HTML contains the fallback text near the Talk section.
close_reason: Phase 3 complete: plain contact fallback added near Talk.

description: Scope: layouts/_partials/home/sidebar.html and, if helpful, content/_index.md front matter. Add a concise fallback line near the Talk card, for example: If booking or chat links do not work, email bogorad@gmail.com or call/text +1-646-827-0546. Current evidence: content/_index.md stores Talk links, and sidebar.html renders the Talk card. Desired state: the fallback contact route is visible in static HTML near Talk. Constraints: do not introduce JavaScript or third-party dependencies; keep copy plain and copyable. Verification: rg "booking or chat|bogorad@gmail.com|646-827-0546" .temp/hugo-public after build finds the fallback in generated output.
close_reason: Plain email/phone fallback added near Talk card.

title: Fix Beads auto-start port 0 failure
description: After migrating Beads into bogorad.github.io, the database and imported issues work after an explicit 'bd dolt start', but normal commands after a stop still fail with 'dolt circuit breaker is open' and 'Port: 0'. Reproduction: (1) run 'bd dolt start', (2) confirm 'bd status' works, (3) run 'bd dolt stop --force', (4) run 'bd status' again. Expected: Beads auto-starts the Dolt server and status succeeds. Actual: Beads reports port 0 and fails fast. Notes: 'bd init -p bogorad-new --database bogorad_github_io --skip-agents --skip-hooks' succeeded, issue import succeeded, remote push succeeded, and 'bd dolt show' reports Host 127.0.0.1, Port 0, Mode per-project when the server is not manually running.
notes:
Rechecking current Beads/Dolt failure. Current evidence points to an invalid Dolt remote URL using git+ssh, not the old port 0 startup failure.
Removed invalid Dolt remote origin from bogorad_github_io. bd dolt pull now succeeds and skips because no remote is configured.
close_reason: Current Beads/Dolt failure was invalid remote configuration, not port 0 startup. Removed the bad origin remote; bd dolt pull now exits successfully with no remote configured.

title: Add GitHub Actions workflow for Hugo Pages deployment
description:
Scope:
- Add a GitHub Actions workflow in .github/workflows/ that builds the Hugo site from repository root and deploys it to GitHub Pages
- Use the standard Pages artifact/deploy pattern rather than committing generated output back into the repo
- Ensure the workflow runs on pushes to main and can also be run manually if useful
- Set required permissions for Pages deployment and define the github-pages environment if needed
- Ensure the workflow installs the correct Hugo version or uses a stable setup action

Checks:
- Workflow validates on push and/or in GitHub Actions UI
- Workflow builds the site from source rather than relying on committed public/
- Deployment artifact contains the generated static site
- The workflow does not depend on old Jekyll/Gemfile tooling

Out of scope:
- Do not switch repository Pages settings inside this task unless needed for testing
- Do not preserve generated public/ in git history
notes: Added .github/workflows/hugo.yml to build and deploy the root-level Hugo site through GitHub Pages artifacts. Latest successful deployment run used commit 4f3bf69. GitHub still emits a Node 20 deprecation warning from the current official Pages actions (configure-pages@v5, deploy-pages@v4, upload-pages-artifact@v4); the workflow is already on the current major versions, so this remaining warning is upstream action runtime behavior rather than repo-local site code.

title: Clone live repo and freeze current site into archive/legacy-site
description:
Scope:
- Clone bogorad/bogorad.github.io into /home/chuck/git/bogorad.github.io using gh repo clone
- Inspect current default branch, Pages settings, and working tree state in the cloned repo
- Create branch archive/legacy-site from the current live main branch before replacing anything
- Push archive/legacy-site so the frozen legacy version exists on GitHub, not only locally
- Do not edit archive/legacy-site after creation except for emergency recovery

Checks:
- Confirm repository remote points to bogorad/bogorad.github.io
- Confirm archive/legacy-site tip matches the pre-migration main commit
- Confirm branch is visible on GitHub after push
- Record the preserved commit SHA in issue notes for rollback reference

Out of scope:
- No replacement-site changes on main yet
- No Pages setting changes yet
notes: Preserved live main commit 7095433 on remote branch archive/legacy-site. Clone created at /home/chuck/git/bogorad.github.io and branch pushed successfully before any main-branch replacement work.

title: Remove legacy Jekyll files from main and keep only intentional replacements
description:
Scope:
- Audit the current bogorad.github.io main branch contents after the Hugo source is staged for migration
- Remove Jekyll-era files and directories from main that are no longer part of the new site, such as _config.yml, _layouts/, _includes/, _sass/, _data/, Gemfile, Gemfile.lock, legacy root HTML files, and other obsolete assets
- Evaluate whether any legacy file should be kept intentionally for the new site (for example favicon or README content) and document that choice explicitly
- Treat the old CNAME carefully: determine whether it is stale and should be dropped or intentionally preserved

Checks:
- Main branch contains only files required by the new Hugo site, deployment workflow, and intentional repo docs/metadata
- No dead Jekyll config remains on main by accident
- Any kept legacy file has a documented reason

Out of scope:
- Do not delete the preserved legacy branch
- Do not change Pages settings in this task

title: Map and move the Hugo site to repository root
description:
Scope:
- Treat the current Hugo site source as the migration input
- Move/adapt the new Hugo site so the live repository root becomes the Hugo project root
- Root-level target structure should include hugo.toml, content/, data/, layouts/, assets/, static/, and any root-level docs or metadata intentionally kept
- Update any repo-relative assumptions that only worked because the site previously lived under bogorad-site/
- Ensure local build commands run from repository root after the move

Checks:
- hugo --panicOnWarning --noBuildLock succeeds from repo root
- hugo server works from repo root
- No source file still depends on the old nested bogorad-site/ path layout
- Root-level structure is documented clearly for future edits

Out of scope:
- No GitHub Pages settings changes in this task
- No deletion of legacy Jekyll files unless required by the root migration work

title: Cut over GitHub Pages from legacy branch publishing to GitHub Actions
description:
Scope:
- Update GitHub Pages settings for bogorad/bogorad.github.io from the current legacy deploy-from-branch mode to GitHub Actions
- Verify the Pages source changes from branch root publishing to Actions-based deployment
- Ensure the first Actions-based deployment targets https://bogorad.github.io/
- Confirm HTTPS remains enforced and no unexpected custom-domain configuration is left active

Checks:
- Pages settings show GitHub Actions as the deployment source
- A deployment run completes successfully and GitHub reports the site as built/deployed
- https://bogorad.github.io/ serves the new Hugo site after cutover
- If a custom domain is not intended, no stale CNAME/domain state remains in effect

Out of scope:
- Do not preserve old branch-root deployment behavior
- Do not modify archive/legacy-site
notes: Switched GitHub Pages from legacy branch-root publishing to workflow mode via the Pages REST API. Verified build_type=workflow, source=main:/, html_url=https://bogorad.github.io/, and https_enforced=true. Main branch pushes cfb3262, fb699f2, and 4f3bf69 deployed successfully.

title: Verify production cutover and rollback readiness
description:
Scope:
- After deployment, verify the live site at https://bogorad.github.io/ on desktop and mobile
- Check that the homepage renders correctly, the main contact links work, the timezone interaction works on touch/mobile, and core assets load successfully
- Confirm the live repo structure matches the intended root-level Hugo source layout
- Confirm archive/legacy-site remains available as a rollback reference
- Document a simple rollback path using the preserved branch and/or revert commits if the cutover fails later

Checks:
- Live desktop render matches expected design closely
- Live mobile render and touch interactions behave as expected
- Major links/icons and generated assets resolve successfully
- archive/legacy-site remains unchanged and accessible
- Rollback instructions are written in issue notes or repo docs

Out of scope:
- No new feature work
- No design changes except break/fix discovered during verification
notes: Production verification passed. Desktop live site title, hero heading, subtitle, and contact order matched expectations. Mobile verification confirmed the timezone bubble appears on tap, shows 'Central European Time', and auto-hides after about 3 seconds. Rollback reference remains archive/legacy-site at 7095433a97b7958b44f8b73b9dab544e148bc7b2. Rollback path: restore main from archive/legacy-site (or revert the migration commits) and push main to trigger a Pages redeploy.

title: Migrate bogorad.github.io from legacy Jekyll setup to root-level Hugo with GitHub Actions
description:
Goal: replace the current live user-site repository bogorad/bogorad.github.io with the new Hugo-based site while preserving the existing public URL https://bogorad.github.io/ and keeping the old site available on a frozen branch named archive/legacy-site.

Target architecture:
- Keep repository name bogorad.github.io unchanged
- Preserve current main branch contents by branching archive/legacy-site from current live main before any destructive changes
- Replace main with the new Hugo site source at repository root (not nested in a subdirectory)
- Use GitHub Actions for Pages build and deploy instead of the current legacy branch-root Pages build
- Treat public/ as local/generated output only; do not deploy from committed public/ contents
- Add gitignore rules so generated Hugo output is not mistaken for deploy input
- Remove old Jekyll-era files from main unless explicitly kept for the new site

Constraints and decisions already made:
- Old site stays as branch archive/legacy-site, not as a renamed or archived repo
- New site should live at repo root, not under bogorad-site/
- GitHub Actions should build on GitHub from source and deploy the resulting artifact
- public/ should be gitignored in the live repo
- Keep the public URL unchanged at https://bogorad.github.io/
- Do not carry forward stale legacy data unless deliberately chosen (example: old CNAME looked stale during earlier inspection)

Definition of done:
- archive/legacy-site exists and preserves the current live site
- main contains only the new Hugo source/layout needed for the replacement site
- legacy Jekyll files are removed from main unless intentionally retained
- GitHub Pages is configured to deploy via GitHub Actions
- A successful Pages deployment publishes the new site at https://bogorad.github.io/
- Local testing instructions remain clear and public/ is ignored in git
- Post-cutover verification confirms desktop/mobile render, major links, and Pages settings
notes: Migration executed successfully in /home/chuck/git/bogorad.github.io. The old live site is preserved on archive/legacy-site, main now contains the root-level Hugo source, GitHub Pages deploys through Actions, and the live URL https://bogorad.github.io/ is serving the new site.

title: Fix missing Hugo scripts partial

title: Remove leftover refactor artifacts

notes: Removed the unused [location] block from content/_index.md, deleted the old static/images/bcn.png source, rebuilt Hugo, and confirmed public/images now contains only profile.jpg and bcn-mini.webp. Also updated REAMDE.md so it no longer mentions the removed PNG or unused content field.

title: Fix timezone icon not showing on page
description: The resized Barcelona timezone icon file is reachable directly, but the icon is not visibly rendering on the homepage. Reproduce against the running local page, identify whether the issue is stale HTML, template output, or CSS/computed style, and fix it without changing the design.
notes: Reproduced manually on http://127.0.0.1:1313 because the site has no browser test harness. Root cause: .contact-links

title: Split homepage into Hugo base and partials
description: Replace the monolithic home template with baseof, home, and partial templates while preserving DOM structure, CSS hooks, and visual output.

title: Move CSS to Hugo assets and verify parity

title: Move homepage content into Hugo content and data
description: Move homepage copy and editable fields out of hugo.toml into content/_index.md and structured data files, preserving the existing rendered content and order.

title: Refactor Hugo site to use Hugo primitives
description: Analyze the existing bogorad-site and refactor it toward idiomatic Hugo structure while preserving the current visual design exactly. Replace monolithic config/template usage with human-editable Hugo primitives where they fit best, such as content files, data files, base templates, partials, and Hugo asset handling where safe. Avoid forcing primitives that add complexity without improving editability. Preserve rendered layout, class hooks, responsive behavior, and visual styling.
notes: Implementation is complete in the current branch/directory. Source refactor builds cleanly and the child subtasks are closed. Parent stays open pending user review and commit decision.

title: Ignore local Hugo build output and local-only artifacts
description:
Scope:
- Update the live repo .gitignore for the root-level Hugo layout
- Ignore /public/ so generated output is not mistaken for deploy input
- Ignore .hugo_build.lock and any other Hugo local build artifacts that should not be committed
- Keep the ignore rules minimal and focused on generated/local files only

Checks:
- git status remains clean after local Hugo builds except for intentional source changes
- public/ is clearly excluded from commits in the live repo
- Ignore rules do not hide real source files by mistake

Out of scope:
- Do not add broad ignore patterns unrelated to the website repo
- Do not remove intentional tracked assets
